channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: SEC just hit four companies with big fines for downplaying the SolarWinds hack
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission fined four companies on Tuesday with misleading investors about the impact the 2020 hack of SolarWinds had on their own systems.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta Oversight Board seriously concerned about Facebook’s ‘overenforcement’ of Harris-Walz nipple post
item.description: Meta Platforms’ Oversight Board on Wednesday told the company to keep up a Facebook post superimposing the faces of U.S.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: We got a sneak peak at the Asus ROG Phone 9, one of the first Snapdragon 8 Elite phones, and it's going to be fast
item.description: Several Android manufacturers have confirmed that their upcoming devices will be powered by the Snapdragon 8 Elite chipset.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Qualcomm’s license battle with Arm puts many AI-enabled Copilot+ PCs in peril
item.description: As its long-running dispute with Arm turned into a war of words this week, the stakes for chip giant Qualcomm and its technology partners, including Microsoft, couldn’t be higher. Along with MediaTek and Apple, Qualcomm is one of the biggest suppliers of chips for use in smartphones and tablets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Anthropic’s new ‘computer use’ ability could further AI automation
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet large language model has gained a new ability: operating a computer. The new ability, which the company is calling “computer use,” is currently in beta test.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: US Banks Face New Open Banking Rule: A Boost to Competition and Consumer Power
item.description: The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau finalizes a rule that will give consumers access to their financial data, aiming to fuel competition and improve services for loans and credit cards.
item.dc:creator: Tribune News Service, The Mercury News

item.title: Excitement growing over GSA’s COMET II program
item.description: GSA is putting the final touches on its next generation IT services contract, called COMET II, that is expected to draw dozens of bidders. The post Excitement growing over GSA’s COMET II program first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The best Python libraries for parallel processing
item.description: Python is powerful, versatile, and programmer-friendly, but it isn’t the fastest programming language around. Some of Python’s speed limitations are due to its default implementation, CPython, being single-threaded. That is, CPython doesn’t use more than one hardware thread at a time.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Mimao mejora la atención al cliente gracias a la integración de sus plataformas digitales
item.description: En la última década, las ventas de productos relacionados con el sector de la moda han experimentado un crecimiento muy significativo en España a través del canal online.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work has begun on JDK 24, with six features so far proposed for the release: removal of the non-generational mode in the Z Garbage Collector, stream gatherers, a vector API, a class-file API, warnings to prepare developers for
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here’s how to stay safe from scams
item.description: As scammers find new ways to steal money and personal information, consumers should be more vigilant about who they trust, especially online.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Flying air taxis move closer to US takeoff with issuing of FAA rule
item.description: Federal regulators are giving a strong push to electric-powered air taxis by issuing a final rule for operating the aircraft and how pilots will be trained to fly them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kamala Harris is going all in on Snapchat. But Donald Trump is still banned
item.description: Since July, Kamala Harris’s campaign has purchased more than 5,000 Snapchat ads, amounting to an estimated $​​5.3 million. It’s not hard to understand why: Snapchat is a haven for young people.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Elon Musk says he’ll fix the government under Trump. His track record paints a different picture
item.description: Elon Musk is making some big promises while campaigning for former President Donald Trump. But his business record calls into question whether he can deliver.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Beware of fake parking tickets on your windshield with fake fines
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how scammers use fake parking ticket texts and receipts to con victims. Learn how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2024 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: xAI Unveils API Service for Grok Models: A New Path in Generative AI
item.description: xAI, a generative AI venture backed by Elon Musk, has launched an API service for its Grok models. This move is aimed at competing with OpenAI and providing developers with access to large language models.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Agile and devops for SaaS and low-code development
item.description: Agile methodologies empower teams to prioritize backlogs, commit to an accomplishable amount of work, and deliver reliable software.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why Python is the language of choice for AI
item.description: The widespread adoption of AI is creating a paradigm shift in the software engineering world. Python has quickly become the programming language of choice for AI development due to its usability, mature ecosystem, and ability to meet the data-driven needs of AI and machine learning (ML) workflows.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Biden administration awards $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for Michigan factory
item.description: The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Meta’s new AI model can check other models’ work
item.description: Facebook owner Meta said on Friday it was releasing a batch of new AI models from its research division, including a “Self-Taught Evaluator” that may offer a path toward less human involvement in the AI development process.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: FTC's rule banning fake online reviews goes into effect
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal rule banning fake online reviews is now in effect. The Federal Trade Commission issued the rule in August banning the sale or purchase of online reviews.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA
item.description: The Spacecraft Cybersecurity Act would require NASA to secure a cybersecurity protection. The post Legislation now calling for spacecraft manufacturers to create cybersecurity plan when working with NASA first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Floating energy platform could change way you get power in future
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains how NoviOcean's hybrid platform uses wind, solar and wave power to generate clean energy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
